title: Paul Thomas Anderson Wins First Oscar for Adapted Screenplay
text: Paul Thomas Anderson claimed his first Academy Award at the 98th Oscars ceremony on March 15, 2026, winning Best Adapted Screenplay for One Battle After Another, a film inspired by novelist Thomas Pynchon's works. The achievement marks a significant milestone for Anderson, who has accumulated 14 nominations throughout his career without previously winning. His first nomination came nearly 30 years ago for Boogie Nights. Upon receiving the award, Anderson expressed his gratitude, stating: I'm incredibly honored to be a part of this history. He also acknowledged his huge debt of admiration to Pynchon for the source material. Anderson revealed the personal motivation behind the screenplay, explaining that he created the film partly as an apology to his children for environmental challenges they will inherit, while also expressing hope that their generation will bring common sense and decency to the world. One Battle After Another stars Leonardo DiCaprio and Teyana Taylor as revolutionary characters who become parents. The ensemble cast includes Sean Penn and Benicio Del Toro, both nominated for supporting actor roles.
